topic: Mobile optimization for the Mismoi website (index.html)

conversation.content: Root issue is architectural -- desktop-first with mobile overrides bolted on. html { font-size: 1.6rem } is the load-bearing problem. Sand-lines 51px/102px line-heights are structural spacers. Proposed two tracks: fluid type scale with clamp(), and fluid spatial system replacing fixed-pixel padding.
Agrees root font-size is the single domino. Ranks fixes: font-size override and CTA line-height break the page. Section padding and container width are cheap. Stats grid and pills are lower priority.
1.6rem makes text-4xl compute to ~57.6px on mobile. iOS Safari font inflation compounds the problem. CTA h2 will have ~48-50px text inside 102px line box.
Priority by user visibility: CTA h2 line-height is a bug. Hero h1 gaps are jarring. Section padding feels empty. Touch targets on service pills only matter if interactive.
Questions whether mobile traffic matters for B2B funnel. Only two fixes urgent: 102px line-height and CTA touch target. Everything else waits for data.
Fix sequence matters. Proposed: freeze root with font-size:1rem at 640px, audit what breaks, replace fixed sand-lines values. Four changes, one hour.
Shipped actual CSS: @media (max-width:640px) with root font-size, sand-lines line-height, section padding, container width. Plus CTA min-height. Called clamp() scope creep.
clamp() with rem units computes against inflated root. iPad Mini loses 25% viewport to padding. Fix order must be sequential.
Reframed as first 3 seconds priority. Hero h1 and CTA h2 are above-fold credibility. Three CSS rules minimum. clamp() is scope creep.
Pushed back on changing root font-size -- intentional design, risks desktop regressions. Surgical component-level overrides only.
Proposed clamp(14px, 1.5vw + 10px, 16px) as single line eliminating breakpoint debate. Component overrides create scattered hacks.
Debate converged. Five rules, ten minutes. Ship. clamp() is second pass.
line-height:1.4 on multi-line headings needs real-device check. CSS specificity risk: sand-lines override could silently fail.
Fix sand-lines and CTA first (undeniable bugs), test before touching root. Root change shrinks everything.
Conceded four-line fix is right. Challenged 640px breakpoint -- 641px gap on tablet portrait. Suggested 768px.
Fix root to 16px at 768px. CTA may be fine once root deflates. Future edits inherit sane coordinates.
Only blocker is specificity. Check DevTools. Ship at 640px. clamp() maintenance cost for solo operator.
Override must match .sand-lines h1/h2 selector weight. CTA min-height could lose to Tailwind. Verify before pushing.
Stick with 640px. Leave root alone. CTA needs visual padding. Ship two surgical fixes.
Android touch target is 48dp not 44px. Audit specificity first. Questions whether 1.6rem was intentional.
Final: Fix root to 16px at 768px. Sand-lines line-height:1.2. CTA 48px with padding. Container 92vw. Dropped clamp().
Final: Three surgical fixes -- sand-lines line-height, CTA 48px, leave root alone. 640px. Verify specificity.
Final: Sand-lines line-height first (unitless 1.2). CTA 48px. Root conditional on audit. Test Chrome Android AND Safari iOS.
Final: Three fixes -- sand-lines, CTA 48px with padding, container 90vw. Root font-size is week-two. 640px breakpoint.
REVERSED: 1.6rem is clearly a mistake. Fix root to 16px FIRST, observe. After deflation CTA py-4 = 64px (fine). Only sand-lines 102px and container 80vw still need fixes.

synthesis: Fix root font-size to 1rem at 768px breakpoint first. Then sand-lines h1/h2 line-height to 1.2 (unitless, matching selector weight). Then container to 92vw. CTA likely self-resolves after root deflation. Verify specificity in DevTools before pushing. Test on real device.
